growth_records.medium: mPGM (modified Postgate's medium; PGM + 25 mM glucose)

up.method: HPLC (refractive index) / GC; F. prausnitzii monoculture produced very small amounts of lactate and fermented little glucose; numeric value only in Fig. 1f, not in text

sec.method: HPLC-RI; monoculture of F. prausnitzii produced very small amounts of lactate; numeric value only in Fig. 1f

growth_records.cite: Khan et al. Synergy and oxygen adaptation for development of next-generation probiotics. Nature (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41586-023-06378-w. [PMC10412450]
growth_records.snippet: monocultures of F. prausnitzii produced very small amounts of lactate (Fig. 1f) ... cultivated as monocultures or co-culture under anaerobic conditions in mPGM medium for 24 h. 'mM change' indicates the difference in concentration from the
growth_records.notes: Batch anaerobic monoculture in fully-defined mPGM (PGM composition given verbatim in Methods; PGM base sodium lactate 3.5 g/L is the F. prausnitzii-relevant carbon/electron source alongside 25 mM added glucose). No growth rate (mu) or doubling time reported; growth was tracked as CFU (Fig. 1e) and metabolites as 'mM change' over 24 h (Fig. 1f). Numeric metabolite values appear ONLY in Fig. 1f and are NOT stated in text, so no qS/qP could be computed without inventing numbers; rates left null. Metabolites quantified by HPLC with refractive-index detection (Reprogel H 9um 250x4.6mm, 0.0025 M H2SO4 eluent) and SCFAs by GC. Routine strain medium is YCFAG; the flux experiment used mPGM.

growth_records.medium: mPGM (modified Postgate's medium; PGM + 25 mM glucose)

up.method: HPLC-RI; co-culture promoted fermentation of glucose (glucose significantly consumed vs monocultures, P=3.1e-6 vs D. piger, 3.8e-6 vs F. prausnitzii); numeric value only in Fig. 1f
HPLC-RI; lactate produced by F. prausnitzii and consumed by D. piger as electron sink; net lactate change reported in Fig. 1f only

sec.method: GC; co-culture produced butyrate (P=1e-6 vs D. piger, 3.1e-6 vs F. prausnitzii) via butyryl-CoA:acetate CoA-transferase; numeric value only in Fig. 1f
HPLC-RI; co-culture promoted lactate production; net value in Fig. 1f only
GC; acetate did NOT accumulate in co-culture (consumed for butyrate production via butyryl-CoA:acetate CoA-transferase) -> net acetate accumulation ~0

growth_records.cite: Khan et al. Synergy and oxygen adaptation for development of next-generation probiotics. Nature (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41586-023-06378-w. [PMC10412450]
growth_records.snippet: co-cultures of F. prausnitzii with D. piger promoted fermentation of glucose and production of lactate and butyrate (Fig. 1f). F. prausnitzii produces butyrate through the butyryl-CoA:acetate CoA-transferase pathway and accordingly, acetate
growth_records.notes: Batch anaerobic CO-CULTURE in defined mPGM, 24 h, 37C, pH 7.2. Growth of F. prausnitzii significantly increased in co-culture vs monoculture (CFU, Fig. 1e, P=0.0003) but no mu/doubling time given. Directional flux is stated in text: glucose consumed (fermented), lactate + butyrate produced, acetate net-unchanged (produced by D. piger from lactate then consumed by F. prausnitzii for butyrate synthesis). Cross-feeding model (Fig. 1g): D. piger consumes lactate (electron sink) -> acetate -> F. prausnitzii uses acetate + glucose -> butyrate. All metabolite magnitudes are Fig. 1f only and absent from text; qS/qP not computable without fabricating numbers, so rates left null except acetate net accumulation ~0 and glucose-uptake=0 cases that are stated in prose. Only P-values are given in text.
